data.title: Dow Jones Futures up, AMD spikes on OpenAI deal. Palantir, Tesla rebound after last week's retreats.
data.description: Stock futures saw an increase in the early hours of Monday. OpenAI announced its plan to acquire AI chips from AMD, causing a spike in AMD shares. Additionally, Palantir and Tesla experienced a recovery from the downturn they faced last week. The Dow Jones is showing positive movement in response to these developments.

data.title: Fed Governor Miran dismisses fears of tariff-induced inflation, stating no evidence they are a 'material driver'. Data shows no significant impact on rising consumer prices. No divergence in inflation between goods subject to import duties and domestically produced goods. Core goods inflation in the US not significantly different from other developed nations without similar tariffs. Miran remains optimistic about the situation.
data.description: In a speech at the MFA Policy Outlook 2025 conference, Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran disputed the common belief that tariffs significantly contribute to inflation. Miran argued that according to the data, tariffs are not a substantial factor driving the increase in consumer prices. He pointed out that there is no notable difference in inflation rates between goods subject to tariffs and domestically produced goods, contradicting the assumption that tariffs impact inflation levels. Additionally, he highlighted that core goods inflation in the U.S. has not diverged significantly from trends in other developed countries that do not have similar tariff policies. Miran emphasized, "I would expect if tariffs were a significant driver of inflation to see a higher rate of inflation than imported core goods. And I do not see that in the data." This perspective plays a pivotal role in Miran's positive outlook on the situation.

data.title: Stock market shows gains with Dow and S&P reaching highs, while Palantir drops due to Army network worries. Shopify rises.
data.description: Today in the stock market, small-cap stocks were the top performers in Friday's trading session. Shopify saw a significant increase, while there was a downturn for several AI stocks, particularly Palantir. The Dow and S&P reached new highs amidst this market activity. Palantir faced a decline in value due to concerns related to its involvement with the Army network.

data.title: Stoner's Pizza expands at Amerant Bank Arena as Official Pizza of Florida Panthers, offering fan-favorite gameday deal.
data.description: In Fort Lauderdale, Florida on October 10, 2025, Stoner's Pizza announced the start of its second season as the Official Pizza of the Florida Panthers. The company has expanded its presence inside Amerant Bank Arena and brought back its popular gameday offer for fans. This move allows fans to access Stoner's Pizza at the arena, amplifying the game-day experience.

data.title: Tequity's client, BookNow Software, acquired by ROLLER in their 28th Salesforce transaction, boosting service capabilities.
data.description: BookNow completes its 28th Salesforce transaction with the acquisition of Tequity, marking a significant milestone for the company. This cross-border acquisition is aimed at boosting innovation in guest experience and strengthening ROLLER's capacity to cater to customers in Europe and the US. The deal was finalized on October 10, 2025, in Toronto. Tequity, recognized as a prominent M&A advisory firm in North America, brings valuable expertise to further enhance BookNow's market position.

data.title: Jewish Book Council introduces Nu Reads, a new subscription book series featuring diverse Jewish literature.
data.description: Renowned authors Maya Arad and Sam Sussman are featured in the inaugural lineup of Nu Reads, a new bi-monthly subscription book series introduced by the Jewish Book Council. The series aims to provide readers with access to exceptional Jewish literature. The announcement was made in New York on October 10, 2025.

data.title: T1 Energy (NYSE: TE) shares rise after investing in Talon PV LLC, developer of solar cell facilities, aiming to produce advanced solar cells.
data.description: Shares of T1 Energy Inc. (NYSE: TE) are up in premarket trading on Friday following the company's acquisition of a minority stake in Talon PV LLC, a solar cell facilities developer. The agreement includes T1 entering a SAFE to acquire a stake in Talon, with talks in progress to further enhance the strategic partnership. Both firms are focused on utilizing advanced manufacturing techniques to create cutting-edge solar cells for the U.S. market. Additionally, T1 is advancing its 5 GW G2_Austin solar cell project.

data.title: The Chop House to open new Alcoa location in spring 2026, part of continued growth in East Tennessee.
data.description: In Alcoa, Tennessee, The Chop House, part of a renowned restaurant group known for Connors Steak & Seafood, is set to open its new location in spring 2026. This expansion in East Tennessee emphasizes The Chop House's dedication to providing exceptional dining experiences. The upcoming venue in Alcoa will boast a luxurious wood-themed interior, offering comfortable seating for guests. This represents a significant step in The Chop House's growth in the region, aligning with their mission of delivering quality dining experiences to customers.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP trading sideways as Dogecoin rises; $197.7m inflow in BTC spot ETFs, $8.5m outflow in ETH, 138,614 traders liquidated for $427.45m in 24 hours. $90.2m in Bitcoin open interest. Will altcoins rally like in 2017/2021? Analysts signal both optimism and pessimism. Bitcoin could drop to $96,000 or even $70,000 in worst-case scenario.
data.description: Bitcoin prices have stabilized after hitting fresh all-time highs last week, with the market experiencing a sell-off sentiment. On Friday, the cryptocurrency market remained subdued as Bitcoin saw minimal movement. Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded inflows of $197.7 million, while spot Ethereum ETFs saw outflows of $8.5 million. Over the past 24 hours, 138,614 traders were liquidated, resulting in a total of $427.45 million in losses. Bitcoin's open interest decreased to $90.2 million but is still close to peak levels. Analysts are debating whether altcoins will mirror the rally seen in 2017 and 2021. El Crypto Prof suggested that altcoins are at a critical juncture, with a potential for a significant rally based on historical trends. However, analyst Ali Martinez warned of a scenario where Bitcoin could face rejection at $124,000, leading to a drop to $96,000 and possibly $70,000. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: ITW to announce Q3 2025 results on Oct 24 at 7:00 a.m. CDT, with earnings webcast at 9:00 a.m. CDT.
data.description: On October 10, 2025, Illinois Tool Works Inc. (NYSE: ITW) announced that it will reveal its third quarter 2025 financial results on October 24, 2025, at 7:00 a.m. CDT, followed by an earnings webcast at 9:00 a.m. CDT on the same day. This disclosure will give investors insight into the company's performance during this period. Keep an eye out for the specific numbers and details that will be shared during the webcast.

data.title: Stock futures rose after market decline, with companies like Delta Air Lines and PepsiCo seeing gains. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.11%. Federal Reserve likely to cut interest rates in October meeting with a 94.6% likelihood. Dow Jones +0.10%, S&P 500 +0.10%, Nasdaq 100 +0.11%, Russell 2000 +0.37%. Applied Digital Corp saw a 25.74% jump in premarket trading after reporting $64.2 million in revenue for its fiscal first quarter.
data.description: U.S. stock futures rose on Friday following a decline the previous day, with major benchmark indices showing higher numbers. Corporate earnings were a positive note, with Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) rising 4.3% after beating estimates and PepsiCo Inc. (NASDAQ: PEP) gaining 4.2% with an adjusted EPS of $2.29. President Trump hinted at discussions on trade issues with China's President Xi Jinping. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.11%, the two-year bond at 3.58%. FedWatch tool reflected a 94.6% likelihood of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates in October. The futures change showed an increase in Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq 100, and Russell 2000. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF rose in premarket trading. Applied Digital Corp. (NASDAQ: APLD) saw a 25.74% increase after reporting $64.2 million in revenue for its fiscal first quarter. USANA Health Sciences also showed a positive trend.
